chapters.title: The Lost Homework
chapters.intro: Maya comes into class and can't find her homework in her bag. She searches and searches, getting flustered. The teacher asks everyone to pass their work forward.
chapters.question: What do you do?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Roll your eyes — she always does this
choices.outcome: Maya catches your eye roll. She already feels terrible — now she feels judged too. She gives up looking.

choices.label: B
choices.outcome: Lying might help today, but it teaches Maya to hide instead of asking for support. The real problem doesn't go away.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Look away so she doesn't feel watched
choices.outcome: You give Maya space. She's still flustered, but at least she doesn't have an audience.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Quietly say 'don't worry, just tell her you'll bring it tomorrow'
choices.outcome: Maya takes a breath. She tells the teacher honestly. The world doesn't end. She remembers your kindness all day.

facts: ADHD affects the brain's ability to organize and manage time. Losing things isn't carelessness — it's a symptom.
chapters.reflection: Think of something you find really hard to remember. What helps you?
chapters.myth: She loses things because she doesn't care
chapters.reality: ADHD affects the brain's organization system — losing things is a symptom, not laziness

chapters.title: Zoning Out
chapters.intro: During the teacher's lesson, you notice Maya staring at the window. She clearly hasn't heard the last instructions. The teacher calls on her.
chapters.question: What do you do?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Smirk — this is going to be funny
choices.outcome: Maya freezes. She doesn't know the answer. Her face burns red. Your smirk just made it ten times worse.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Quickly whisper the answer to her
choices.outcome: Maya gives the answer. The lesson moves on. She shoots you a grateful look — you just saved her from a really hard moment.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Nudge her — she should be paying attention
choices.outcome: She jumps, surprised. Everyone notices. Now the whole class is looking at her, and she still doesn't know the answer.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nothing — she needs to learn to listen
choices.outcome: Maya freezes. The silence stretches. Listening isn't the problem — her brain just works differently.

facts: ADHD makes sustained attention very difficult, especially for tasks that aren't immediately stimulating. This is not a choice.
chapters.reflection: Have you ever 'zoned out' during a long lesson? What pulled you back?
chapters.myth: She zones out because she chooses not to listen
chapters.reality: ADHD makes sustained attention genuinely difficult — the effort is invisible but real

chapters.title: Blurting Out
chapters.intro: During a class discussion, Maya shouts out an answer before the teacher finishes the question. Some kids groan. The teacher asks students to raise hands.
chapters.question: What do you do?

choices.label: A
choices.outcome: Maya hears the groans. She gets quiet for the rest of the class. She had good ideas she'll never share.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Give her a look like 'what are you doing'
choices.outcome: Your look tells her she doesn't belong in this conversation. She starts to believe it.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Raise your hand to continue the discussion normally
choices.outcome: You move the lesson forward. Maya isn't singled out. The class keeps going, and so does she.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defend her loudly: 'Leave her alone everyone'
choices.outcome: Now everyone is focused on Maya — for being defended. She's the center of attention again, which is exactly what she didn't want.

facts: Impulsivity — speaking before thinking — is one of the core symptoms of ADHD. It is not rudeness.
chapters.reflection: Have you ever said something before you finished thinking? What did you wish people did next?
chapters.myth: She blurts out answers because she's rude
chapters.reality: Impulsivity is a core ADHD symptom — her brain acts before the 'wait' signal arrives
